article_title: Anthropic Leases the Full Capacity of SpaceX's Colossus 1 Data Center, Adding 300 MW and Over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs to the Claude Backend
summary: Substantively sound — every fact-claim verified. Two Musk quotes attributed to Yahoo Finance cannot be verified from the snapshot (Yahoo redirected to consent gate); Tom's Hardware paraphrases similar content with different wording. Filed as a corrections clarification.

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)
Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: Anthropic Leases the Full Capacity of SpaceX's Colossus 1 Data Center, Adding 300 MW and Over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs to the Claude Backend
content_preview.summary: The May 6 deal hands Anthropic all of xAI's Memphis supercomputer and unlocks immediate rate-limit increases for Claude Code, Claude Pro, Max, and the Opus API.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX to take over the full compute capacity of Colossus 1, the Memphis, Tennessee supercomputer that xAI built and operated, in an arrangement announced on May 6 that gives Claude immediate access to more than 300 megawatts of new capacity and over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs, [according to Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/higher-limits-spacex). The capacity will come online "within the month" of the announcement, the company said. As part of the ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight 516-word News piece. Clear separation of Anthropic-confirmed facts, Musk's framing, and explicit hedging on the orbital-compute strand and unknown financial terms.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 4 snapshots from disk. Anthropic announcement (source-0) confirms 300 MW / 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs / 'within the month' verbatim, 'multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity' verbatim, doubling Claude Code 5-hour rate limits across Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise verbatim, peak-hours removal verbatim, raising API rate limits for Claude Opus verbatim. Tom's Hardware (source-1) confirms Memphis location, Colossus 1/Colossus 2, 'evil detector' Musk quote verbatim, Anthropic-as-rival framing. Al Jazeera (source-2) confirms Memphis/220,000/300 MW, 'evil detector' verbatim, Dario Amodei attribution, Musk-OpenAI lawsuit context. Yahoo Finance (source-3) redirected to consent.yahoo.com — content unverifiable from snapshot.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All Anthropic-attributed facts verified verbatim. Musk's 'evil detector' line verified verbatim across two snapshots. Two specific Musk quotes attributed to Yahoo Finance ('Everyone I met was highly competent and cared a great deal about doing the right thing'; 'SpaceXAI had already moved training to Colossus 2') cannot be verified from the snapshot — Tom's Hardware paraphrases similar but not identical content. The substance (Musk vetting Anthropic leadership, training already moved off Colossus 1) is corroborated; the specific verbatim wording is not.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and well-hedged. Distinguishes 'expressed interest' orbital intent from binding plan. No editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: First-of-cluster review for Anthropic/Colossus deal in this batch (PRs #1197 and #1199 cover the same announcement and will be rejected as duplicates downstream).
concerns: Two Musk quotes attributed to Yahoo Finance cannot be verified from local snapshot (Yahoo consent gate). The substance is plausibly real and corroborated by Tom's Hardware in paraphrastic form, but the verbatim wording cannot be confirmed.
recommendations: When attributing direct quotes to a paywalled or consent-gated source, prefer corroborating verbatim quotes from at least one openly-fetchable source.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound. The two Yahoo-attributed quotes are unverifiable from snapshot but plausibly real; a corrections clarification preserves auditability while the article publishes.
